free_text: Neutron elastic and inelastic scattering differential
cross sections on carbon
Single-ended 7 MV model CN Van de
Graaff accelerator with a terminal-based bunching
system was used in this experiment.
Natural C sample with diameter of 2.01 cm, height of
2.01 cm and mass of 10.98 g was used. For calibration
purposes several other samples were used
Sample        Mass     Diameter      Height
(g)        (cm)         (cm)
polyethylene  1.069     1.94          1.49
Neutrons were detected with an 11.5 cm
diameter x 2.5 cm thick C6D6 main detector. The same
type of detector with thickness of 5.0 cm was used in
runs with d-d neutrons. Detector was mounted on
goniometer which covered angles up  to 155 deg and
flight path up to 4 m.
The sample was placed at distances 6.4 to 7.7 cm
from the center of neutron source gas cell.
3H+p reaction was used for neutrons below 4.5 MeV
D+D reaction was used for neutrons above 4.5 MeV
Compiled by S.H.
Different data obtained in multiple experiments
over the span of approx. 10 years
Total uncertainty consisting of statistical
and systematic uncertainty
